We are seeking a motivated and curious individual to join Flight Centre Travel Group as a Senior BI Analyst.
In this role, you will contribute to the evolution of our analytics capability by helping the organisation make better use of data, advanced analytics, and emerging AI tools. While the BI function traditionally focuses on reporting and dashboards, this position supports the next stage of our analytics journey by enabling new ways for the business to explore data, generate insights, and make more informed decisions.
Working closely with business stakeholders, BI developers, data engineers, and data scientists, you will play a key role in translating strategic business challenges into practical data, analytics, and AI-enabled solutions.
A key part of the role will involve preparing and modelling well-structured datasets that support both traditional BI reporting and modern AI-driven data interaction. This includes developing curated and semantically meaningful data models that make it easier for business users and AI tools to interpret and explore trusted enterprise data.
You will also enable new ways for teams to interact with data, including developing Genie Rooms within Databricks that allow business users to query curated datasets using natural language. In addition, you will support the adoption of AI-assisted analytical workflows using tools such as Claude Enterprise, helping teams accelerate analysis, generate insights, and improve knowledge sharing.
Through this work, the role helps the organisation move beyond traditional reporting towards more insight-driven and AI-assisted decision making.

Join our global team in Tokyo, Japan and shape the future of travel in Japan with Discova as a Product Manager. You will be designing innovative products that deliver authentic experiences and strong business growth!

Flight Centre Travel Group is one of the world’s largest travel companies with over 12,000 employees globally - who we call ‘Flighties’. With over thirty brands under our family tree and headquarters in more than twenty countries, our roles are diverse and our opportunities are endless. When, we say we’re a great place to work, we have the proof to back it up. Over the past 12 years we’ve been voted a ‘Great Place to Work’ including one of the Best Workplaces for Wellbeing and Best Workplaces for Women. Together, we live and breathe our number one philosophy, our people, and turn up each day driven by our purpose to open up the world for those who want to see. Put simply, we’re a bunch of travel lovers looking for more travel lovers to join our family.

t: Business Development Manager- North Sydney, NSW
su:
As our Business Development Specialist, you'll be the key driver of Stage and Screen's growth in the sports and entertainment travel market. You'll strategically identify and pursue new business opportunities, building a robust portfolio of clients across sports teams, entertainment acts, and industry leaders. Your role will blend strategic outreach, relationship building, and sales expertise – connecting with potential clients through targeted communication channels and transforming initial contacts into long-term partnerships.

You'll leverage your communication prowess to engage stakeholders at all levels, from emerging talents to established industry giants. Working both independently and as part of our dynamic team, you'll be instrumental in developing proactive selling strategies, generating quality leads, and consistently meeting ambitious growth targets.
This isn't just a sales role – it's an opportunity to become a critical connection point in the sports and entertainment travel ecosystem.
